series_metadata.series_description: Including any supporting documentation created and/or used to justify and support legislative appropriations requests by an agency.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention
period.

series_metadata.series_description: Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and internal correspondence, in any media, including electronic communication, pertaining to or arising from the routine operations of the policies, programs, services, or projects of an agency.

tracking_data.comments:
Review after expiration of retention
period for continuing administrative
value.

series_metadata.series_description: Calendars, appointment books or programs and scheduling, or itinerary records maintained by staff during business hours that document appointments, itineraries, and other activities of agency officials or employees.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected officials,
agency heads, and board or
commission members require
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_metadata.series_description: News, press releases, marketing files and collateral, or any public relations files maintained or issued by an agency. Includes print, electronic, audio, and audiovisual records.

Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are furnished to the public under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552, Government Code). Also includes withdrawn requests.
series_metadata.legal_citation: Government Code). Also includes withdrawn requests.

series_metadata.series_description: Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are excepted under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552, Government Code), including records provided to the legislature under section 552.008.
series_metadata.legal_citation: Government Code), including records provided to the legislature under section 552.008.

series_metadata.series_description: Charts or diagrams that show the structure of an agency and the relationships and relative ranks of its departments and job positions.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
to the Archives and Information
Services Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are
not considered archival.

series_metadata.series_description: Plans and records relating to the process of planning new or redefined programs, services, or projects of an agency that are neither included in nor directly related to other records series in this schedule.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_metadata.series_description: Copies of all proposed, withdrawn, emergency, and adopted rules; open meetings notices; or any other documents required by law to be submitted to the Texas Register.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Drafts of proposed legislation and related correspondence.
Surveys soliciting feedback from customers, clients, or stakeholders of an agency, and the statistical data maintained rating an agency's performance.

tracking_data.comments:
Review after expiration of retention
period for continuing administrative
value.

series_metadata.series_description: Notes or text of speeches, papers, presentations, or reports delivered in conjunction with agency work.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_metadata.series_description: Records created by or on behalf of an agency in anticipation of or in the adjudication of a lawsuit.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission for
archival preservation. See page ix
for more information.

series_metadata.series_description: Logs or similar records used to register persons appearing before state agencies as required by Chapter 2004, Government Code, including quarterly reports filed with the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).
series_metadata.legal_citation: Government Code, including quarterly reports filed with the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).

series_metadata.series_description: Operational strategic plans, which include descriptions of information resources programs, prepared in accordance with Government Code, 2054.095 and 2056.002. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating the final plan.
series_metadata.legal_citation: Government Code, 2054.095 and 2056.002. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating the final plan.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_metadata.series_description: Self-evaluations and plans documenting compliance with the requirements of the Americans with Disabilities Act.

series_metadata.series_title: Meeting, Agendas and Minutes of Open
series_metadata.series_description: Agendas and minutes of state boards, committees, commissions, and councils that conduct open meetings as required by Chapter 551, Government Code.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement (see Texas
Government Code, Section
324.008(d)) will be met by sending
a copy to the Archives and
Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives
Commission.

series_metadata.series_title: Meetings, Agendas and Minutes or Audiovisual Recordings of Closed
series_metadata.series_description: Agendas, minutes, or audiovisual recordings of closed meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Audiovisual recordings of open meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.
CAUTION: Minutes of state agencies
are permanent records. Agency
treats audio and videotapes as
permanent media. State agencies
may not retain audio or videotapes
of the meetings of governing bodies
in lieu of written minutes. The
proceedings of all meetings of state
boards, committees, commissions,
and councils must be reduced to
writing. SEE comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_metadata.series_description: Documents submitted at meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils, including exhibit items, documentation for agenda items, public comment forms, etc. Includes documents sent in advance of meetings for briefing purposes, some of which may not be submitted at an actual meeting.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Minutes or notes, and supporting documentation taken at internal agency staff meetings.

series_metadata.series_title: Reports and Studies (Non- Fiscal) - Raw Data
series_metadata.series_description: Information or data collected and compiled for the purpose of producing non- fiscal reports.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data included in or
directly related to another records
series in this schedule. SEE
especially item number 1.1.064.

series_metadata.series_description: Biennial narrative reports to the governor and legislature as required by an agency's enabling statutes, including annual narrative reports if they are required by statute.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_metadata.series_description: Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules, or similar records distributed internally for the use of employees or externally to the public or those individuals or entities regulated by an agency that sets out the rules, policies, and procedures that govern an agency's programs, services, or projects. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating rules, policies, and procedures.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and
procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_metadata.series_description: Reports made to the Office of Attorney General (OAG) on an agency's Public Information Act activities, per Government Code, 552.010.

Transcripts and final decisions of hearings conducted as part of the regulatory process, and hearings on proposed rules and changes. The records may be maintained with related information including meeting notices, proofs of publication, and meeting minutes.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only records that
lead to substantial or statutory
changes to the operations of the
agency and its policies and
procedures should be retained for
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.
CAUTION: These records may be
maintained with related
information, including meeting
notices, proofs of publication, and
meeting minutes, and require a
longer retention period.

series_metadata.series_title: Sunset Review Report and Related Documentation
series_metadata.series_description: Sunset Review Report, agency self-study, and other correspondence and supporting documentation related to the Sunset review process for state agencies.

tracking_data.comments:
Review after expiration of retention
period for continuing administrative
value. ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
response to the commission's draft
report and documentation of the
agency's implementation of the
commission's final
recommendations. The final version
of the agency self-evaluation report
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(1).

series_metadata.series_description: Master versions of all forms used internally and externally by the agency, including subsequent revisions to an agency form or any associated design or design modification requests.

A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR 105) that identifies the records that are created and maintained by an agency, provides the minimum timeframes the records must be retained, and includes instructions for their disposition. This series may include working files and documentation of certification and approval by the Texas State Library and Archives Commission.

tracking_data.comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by
the State and Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_metadata.series_description: Forms used to track the transmittal of records to/from onsite and offsite storage areas or a transfer of physical custody.
Logs or similar records listing records disposed of by an agency, which might include records series title, dates of records, and date of disposition.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_metadata.series_description: Worksheets or working papers used to capture records inventory information, including location tracking and to document existence and requirements of a records series.
Documentation used to provide and improve access to records. Includes indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers, guides, and other finding aids.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_metadata.series_description: Relatively long-lived computer files containing organized and consistent sets of complete and accurate electronic records and associated processing files. Examples include, but are not limited to, data tables of relational databases used by applications or computer programs.

tracking_data.comments:
Completion of 3rd verification cycle,
or audit completion confirming
successful transaction updates, or
required audit trail maintenance, or
the ability to restore or migrate
when errors are detected or when
hardware software changes occur.
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of AC
to agency master files and
application data that the files do
not fall under records series listed
elsewhere.

series_metadata.series_description: Agency-developed automated software applications, operating system files, and associated processing files, including job control language, programs, applications, scripts, source code, etc.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting operational and maintenance requirements of computer hardware such as operating manuals, hardware/operating system requirements, hardware configurations, equipment control systems, and associated processing files.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Hardware needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_metadata.series_description: Records adequate to specify all technical characteristics necessary for reading or processing electronic records; to document modifications to computer programs; to access, retrieve, manipulate, and interpret data in an automated system; and allow for their timely, authorized disposition. Records include but are not limited to: user guides, system or sub-system definitions, system specifications, input and output specifications, system flow charts, program flow charts, program maintenance logs, change notices, data element dictionaries, file layouts, code books or tables, and other records that explain the meaning, purpose, structure, logical relationships, and origin of the data elements.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Hardware and software
needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the
period of time required to access
the records.

series_metadata.series_description: Forms and logs used to enter and reconcile data sets submitted for processing.

Information verifying the quality of system, hardware, or software operations. Records include but are not limited to: records of errors or failures and the loss of data resulting from such failures; documentation of abnormal termination and of error-free processing; checks of changes put into production; transaction histories; and other records needed as an audit trail to evaluate data accuracy.

series_metadata.series_title: Software Registrations, Warranties, and License Agreements
series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the registration and licensing of a software application to activate the software for legal use by the end users of a state agency, along with warranties providing that the software will perform in accordance with functional specifications.

Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference, and similar documents whose submission by candidates for vacant positions is required on the application form, by application procedures, or in the employment advertisement.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum

series_metadata.series_description: Agency copies of information relating to the selection by employees of life, disability, health, and other types of insurance or benefits offered by the State of Texas to its employees.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for item
number 3.2.001.

series_metadata.series_description: Internal or external announcements or advertisements of job openings, promotions, training programs, or opportunities for overtime.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Records relating to the review of employee grievances or complaints against personnel policies, working conditions, etc.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the
U.S. Department of Labor (DOL).
SEE item number 1.1.048.

series_metadata.series_description: Job evaluations, performance appraisals, or other similar documents used to evaluate the performance of an employee.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Disciplinary actions are those actions which may affect pay, status, or tenure. They are imposed to discipline an employee whose conduct is harmful to the best interests of the state, the agency, or the employee work force; and for failure to improve performance or conduct following imposition of corrective action. May include cause for demotion, suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons for failure to give written notice of resignation. May also be used to document evidence of employee self-improvement efforts, as well as favorable and unfavorable communication.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Forms or similar records used to create or change information concerning the records of an employee including, pay grade, position classification, employee number, evaluation date, and termination of employment.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Job descriptions, including all associated task or skill statements, for positions in an agency.
Criminal history record information on job applicants or agency employees obtained from the Department of Public Safety (DPS).

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_metadata.series_description: Certificates of completion, certifications, licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar records documenting the training, testing, certification, licensing, or continuing education achievements of an employee.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9).

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Employee or applicant requests for reasonable accommodation under the ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act).
Documentation used to start, modify, or stop all voluntary or required deductions from payroll, including garnishment or other court-ordered attachments.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Payroll records and registers documenting employee earnings, wages, and pay. This may include but is not limited to payroll input records, summary statements, payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and payroll history.
Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax records.
Used to make increases or decreases to employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or in the computation of taxes.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Employer's copy of the "Employee's Withholding Exemption Certificate."
Records defining the wage or salary rate for each position in the agency expressed in dollars, grades, or step numbers.
Records and documentation relating to unemployment compensation claims, including reimbursement of funds disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission (TWC).
Forms used to deposit employee’s earnings into a specified personal account.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency uses all applicable AC
triggers from State RRS.

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the amount of pension or deferred compensation earned by individual employees.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Employee benefit plans such as pension; life, health, and disability insurance; deferred compensation; etc., including amendments.
Reports providing statistical information on labor force.
Records relating to review and monitoring of job classifications within an agency.
Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules, rosters, or assignments.
Reports to the agency from TWC or its predecessor pertaining to employees.

Requests and authorizations for travel; participation in educational programs, workshops, or college classes; or for other work-related activities for which the expenses of the employee are defrayed or reimbursed.
Any internally distributed manuals, guidelines, or similar records that define agency wide policies and procedures concerning the personnel of an agency.
Any documents detailing the procedural duties and responsibilities of agency positions on a position-by-position basis.

Any reports compiled by an agency on aspects of personnel staffing, including listings of all staff by program or name, staff hired during a month, detailed listings of employees within its organizational structure, position vacancies, analysis of turnover rates and seasonality of employment, etc.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Aptitude or skills tests required of job applicants or of current personnel to qualify for promotion or transfer.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each
different test (different in terms of
either questions or administration
procedures) should be retained for
the period indicated.

series_metadata.series_description: Includes documentation used to complete EEO (Equal Employment Opportunity) reports.

Used to create employee leave balances, to transfer leave balances when an employee transfers positions, to adjust carry-over balances on August 31, to correct errors on leave accumulation, and to close out leave accounts on separated employees.
Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle and provides employee leave status information for each position.

Records documenting individual employee’s hours worked, including work schedules and documentation evidencing adherence to or deviation from normal hours for those employees working on fixed schedules.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_title: Time Off and/or Sick Leave Reports
series_metadata.series_description: Requests and authorizations for vacation, compensatory, sick, Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and other types of authorized leave, and supporting documentation.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting number of hours transferred in and out of comprehensive sick leave pool.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Agency policy set retention
period longer than TSLAC minimum.

series_metadata.series_description: Claims; invoices; statements; copies of checks and purchase orders; expenditure authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance, purchase, general journal, expenditure, and special vouchers; and similar records that serve to document disbursements, including those documenting claims for and reimbursement to employees for travel and other expenses.

Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing registers, account cards, deposit warrants, cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts books, cash transfers, daily cash reports, reconciliations, general journal vouchers, special vouchers, and similar records that serve to document money owed to or received by a state agency and its collection or receipt.
Internal and external periodic fiscal management reports and associated worksheets, including federal financial reports, salary reports, HUB reports, investment performance reports, etc.
Annual Financial Reports (AFR) required by the General Appropriations Act (100 Day Report).

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Annual Financial Reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(a). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_metadata.series_description: Required by the General Appropriations Act.
Periodic reports compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
Yearly report compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
Records documenting the internal and external procedural requirements with respect to the accounting department of a state agency.
Records of accounts deemed uncollectible, including write-off authorizations and returned checks, warrants, and drafts.

Documentation relating to the capital and fixed assets of a state agency, including equipment or property history cards containing data on initial cost, depreciation schedules or summaries used for capital outlay budgeting, and property sale, auction, or disposal records of agency owned equipment and property.
Signature cards or similar records establishing authority of an agency employee to initiate or authorize financial transactions on behalf of an agency.
Any mailing address, telephone or fax number, or e-mail address records maintained by an agency on its employees or on entities or persons it serves.

tracking_data.comments:
Review after expiration of retention
period for continuing administrative
value.

series_metadata.series_description: Records and reports of postage expenses, including postal meter usage.

series_metadata.series_title: Requisitions for In-Agency/ Inter-Agency Copy/Printing Service
series_metadata.series_description: Includes word processing and data processing.

series_metadata.series_title: Photocopier and Telefax Usage Logs & Reports
series_metadata.series_description: Registers or logs of print copies and fax transmissions made by user or in total.
Schedules of prices charged by an agency for services to the public or other agencies, including any documentation used to determine the charges.

Liability, theft, fire, health, life, a u tomobile, and other policies for government property and personnel including supporting documentation relevant to the implementation, modification, renewal, or replacement of policies. Executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015

Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile, and other policies for government property and personnel including supporting documentation relevant to the implementation, modification, renewal, or replacement of policies. Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_metadata.series_description: Any internally distributed manual, guidelines, or similar records that establish standard office procedures for an agency; for e.g. agency style manuals, telephone protocols, mail room procedures, print shop and photocopy ordering instructions.
Any record created by an agency to track any type of incoming and outgoing correspondence or packages by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) or by private couriers.
List of agency contracts, leases, and agreements, including general obligation, land lease, utilities, and construction contracts.

Records documenting building space requests and approvals. Information on request forms may include but is not limited to: the building and room; the reason for the change; parties involved in the request; date of request and approval.
Reports summarizing efficiency of facility space utilization, which may include data on room usage, demand, allocation, and capacity.

Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, awarded and unawarded bids, and bid tabulation /evaluations. Associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.

Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, awarded and unawarded bids, and bid tabulation /evaluations. Associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_metadata.series_description: Invalid bids that do not meet agency submission requirements and are not included in bid evaluation process (e.g. withdrawn, missed submission deadline, incomplete submission, etc.)
Log, register, etc., providing a record of purchase orders issued, orders received, and similar data on procurement status.

series_metadata.series_title: Occupational Accident Reports and Associated Documentation
series_metadata.series_description: Accident or occupational disease reports (by supervisors and employees) and other associated reports required to be submitted to the State Office of Risk Management (SORM) or its predecessors or maintained internally on accident frequency.

tracking_data.comments:
The Workers' Compensation
division of the Texas Department of
Insurance retains copies of the
reports submitted to it for 50 years.

series_metadata.series_description: Plans for evacuation of agency facilities in cases of emergency.

Fire, safety, and emergency drill, alarm, and other inspection records of agency facilities and equipment, including orders issued by inspectors to correct deficiencies in compliance with any code or regulations.
Plan that provides information to employees about the hazardous chemicals to which they may be exposed to in their workplace.
List of each hazardous chemical normally present in the workplace.
Logs, registers, or similar records documenting visitors to limited access or restricted areas of agency facilities.

Records relating to the issuance of keys, identification cards, building passes, passwords, signed statements or similar instruments of access to agency or state facilities, equipment or automated systems.
Reports of accidents to adults on state property or in any other situation in which the state agency could be a party to a lawsuit.
Reports of accidents to minors on state property or in any other situation in which the state agency could be a party to a lawsuit.
Documentation relating to disputed long distance calls, including documents evidencing repayment by employees for personal long distance use.
Records generated pursuant to Section 4007.052 (13-1)
Records generated pursuant to Section 4007.052 (13-1) or as part of an investigation - 4007.053 - 4007.058 (28.A).
Records generated pursuant to Chapter 4003.
Records generated pursuant to Section 4005.019 (former Section 5.O).
Records generated pursuant to Rule 109.13.(k) and Rule 114.4.(b)(1).
Records generated pursuant to Rules 139.14, 139.16, 139.19, and 139.26.
Records generated pursuant to Rule 114.4(a)(1).

series_metadata.series_title: Dealers, Agents, Investment Advisers, Representatives Files
series_metadata.series_description: All records relating to a registered entity or individual registration.

series_metadata.series_title: Dealers and Investment Advisers Registration History
series_metadata.series_description: Examples of these records would include registration history information maintained by the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority's Central Registration Depository and the Investment Adviser Registration Depository systems for entities and individuals licensed in Texas.
Internal examination drafts leading to the final examination plan.
Records generated pursuant to Rules 133.29 and 133.9.

series_metadata.series_title: Staff Services Daily Money Batch Numbers
series_metadata.series_description: Log of physical checks received.
Refunds of monies received by the agency in error; for example renewal fee paid by a registrant in error, overpayment of securities fees.
Formal investigation of unregistered securities offerings and their promoters to determine compliance with the Securities Act.

tracking_data.comments:
Archival review code removed
subsequent to appraisal by the
Archives and Information Services
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, April 18,
2012.

series_metadata.series_description: Preliminary investigation of unregistered offerings and their promoters to determine jurisdiction under the Securities Act and to determine if additional resources should be dedicated to the matter and the matter elevated to a formal investigation.

tracking_data.comments:
Archival review code removed
subsequent to appraisal by the
Archives and Information Services
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, April 18,
2012.

series_metadata.series_description: Investigation of licensed firms and individuals. E.g. investigation based upon an investor complaint or disclosure information made known to the agency.

series_metadata.series_title: Complaints on Registered Entities and Individuals
series_metadata.series_description: Dealers/Agents/Investment Advisers and Investment Adviser Representatives
Rules and regulations change/amendment records.

series_metadata.series_title: Daily Disbursement Detail for Dealers, Investment Advisers and Agents
series_metadata.series_description: Daily fee receipts of funds paid directly to the CPA.
Electronic database of individuals and entities subject to investigation or enforcement action.
Electronic database of entities subject to inspection, complaints, agent reviews, legal referrals, and inspections completed.
Documentation of undertakings, notices of hearing, proof of service, administrative fines, and administrative disciplinary orders.
Documentation of requests, reviews, and actions taken on dealer, agent, investment adviser, and investment adviser registration expungement matters.
Records relating to instances where the agency acts as the registered agent for a third-party.
